# Transcrição: Are_you_confident__Or_Contextually_confident_

- **Fonte:** Are_you_confident__Or_Contextually_confident_
- **Duração:** 01:23
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:23]

A pianist who plays piano and plays really well, when do you think they are the most confident? When they're in the... with their piano, right? So you meet a pianist, they'll play piano, it's like... They move like crazy, right? And then they'll move away from the piano, and then what happens to their confidence? They lose it. Because their confidence is attached to the piano. Same thing happened with me. Most of you know I do magic. I used to be so confident when I had a packet of cards. But if I didn't have a packet of cards and I met you all, I'd have no confidence whatsoever. My confidence was attached to my magic. And it became a huge problem for me socially, because every time I saw people and I met people for the first time, I thought all my value was in my cards. So I was like, oh yeah, can I show you some magic? And I became like a trick monkey for people. What's interesting is I can't always have a packet of cards. The pianists cannot always have their instrument. But guess what? You travel everywhere with an instrument. Your voice. Everywhere you go, you bring your voice. So the moment you master this instrument, and you become masterful with it, you have power with your voice. No matter where you go now, you know you have this. Contextual confidence. Now you're confident in every single context of your life. So when you train your voice and you learn to use it well and masterfully, you now have an instrument you carry everywhere. And everywhere you go, you have that confidence.
